era_context: Middle Bronze Age. The Old Babylonian period in Mesopotamia is entering its final phase; the Middle Kingdom in Egypt remains stable under Amenemhat III; the Minoan civilization continues to flourish in the Aegean; the Hittite Old Kingdom is consolidating power in Anatolia. This is a period of relative international stability, though regional conflicts persist.

events.title: Samsu-iluna's Military Campaigns Against the Kassites
events.description: Samsu-iluna conducts military campaigns against the Kassites and other neighboring powers in the Zagros Mountains and surrounding regions. These campaigns are part of a broader pattern of conflict that characterizes the later Old Babylonian period. The Kassites, an Indo-European people, are gradually encroaching on Babylonian territory. These military efforts are ultimately unsuccessful in halting the long-term decline of Old Babylonian power.

sources.name: Cuneiform administrative records mentioning military campaigns

events.certainty_note: Samsu-iluna's military campaigns are attested in general terms; specific year-by-year details are rarely preserved. This event is assigned to this year by chronological convention.

events.title: Amenemhat III's Reign Nears Its End
events.description: Amenemhat III is in the final years of his long reign (he dies c. 1814 BCE). The Middle Kingdom remains stable and prosperous. His successor, Amenemhat IV, is likely already co-regent or preparing for succession. The transition of power is managed smoothly, reflecting the institutional stability of the 12th Dynasty.
events.certainty_note: Amenemhat III's reign is well-attested; the exact year of his death is uncertain (±5–10 years), but he is known to have reigned ~46 years.

events.title: Minoan Palace Culture at Its Height
events.description: The Minoan palaces at Knossos, Phaistos, and Malia are centers of sophisticated administrative, religious, and economic activity. The palaces feature advanced architectural design, including multi-story structures, sophisticated water management systems, and elaborate frescoes depicting religious ceremonies, nature scenes, and daily life. This period represents the apex of Minoan cultural achievement before the Late Minoan period.
events.certainty_note: The Minoan palace culture is well-attested archaeologically; specific year-by-year events are not documented.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific disconfirming evidence identified for events listed. However, the interpretation of Minoan society as peaceful has been questioned by some scholars who point to evidence of violence and warfare in the archaeological record. The dating of Amenemhat III's reign remains subject to ±50-year uncertainty depending on the chronological system used.
historiographic_note: This year falls within a well-documented period for Egypt and Mesopotamia, but year-by-year specificity is limited. The Minoan civilization is known primarily through archaeology. Chronological uncertainty persists, particularly for absolute dating. The historical record is heavily biased toward elite activities and toward regions with writing systems or intensive archaeological investigation.

graph_edges.note: Amenemhat III's reign continues, nearing its end.
